extraction.one_line_summary: DAINESE Alberto won chaotic sprint after late crash at sub-500m eliminated GROVES and exposed absence of dominant leadout train.
tactical_patterns: Without cohesive leadout trains, sprint chaos at 200m caused multi-team collision when riders leaned into wind shelter.
ROGLIC admitted post-Stage 17 team orders constrain him from attacking KUSS despite clear dissatisfaction with helper role.
SEVCIK team protected KUSS on climbs for first time all Vuelta, contrasting earlier every-man-for-himself approach on Tourmalet.
EF rider shoulder-checking while drifting caused pile-up, revealing fatigue-induced positional errors on Stage 19 of GT.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: publicly stated 'I have my own thoughts' about team orders, signaling internal tension despite committing not to attack KUSS
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: received organized climbing support from SEVCIK for first time, marking tactical shift after Stage 17 summit
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: touched wheels at second position sub-500m despite strong form, exposing positioning vulnerability in chaotic sprints
rider_signals.POGACAR Filip: negative: launched sprint too early without leadout, nearly won but faded in final 50m against DAINESE
rider_signals.OLIVEIRA Nelson: negative: crashed earlier in stage on previously injured elbow, eliminating MILANO's primary leadout option
